_note: Yellow Turban Rebellion begins — text states 'first year of Central Stability (AD 184)'. Three Brothers' oath, initial battles.
Yellow Turbans suppressed (184), Zhang Fei whips Du You, then jumps to Emperor Ling's death (May 189) and He Jin's plot against the eunuchs.
Eunuchs kill He Jin; Yuan Shao slaughters them; Dong Zhuo enters Luoyang; Lu Bu betrays Ding Yuan. All within 189.
Dong Zhuo deposes Emperor Bian (first day of 9th month, AD 189) and installs Liu Xie as Emperor Xian. Cao Cao's failed assassination + flight + Lu Boshe killing.
Coalition forms against Dong Zhuo (1st month of Chuping era, 190); battles at Sishui Pass and Hulao Pass; Three Brothers vs Lu Bu.
Dong Zhuo burns Luoyang and relocates court to Changan (2nd–4th month 190). Sun Jian finds the imperial seal in the ruins; coalition disbands.
Yuan Shao seizes Jizhou from Han Fu (191); Pan River battle vs Gongsun Zan (winter 191); Sun Jian dies attacking Liu Biao at Xiangyang (early 192).
Wang Yun and Diao Chan's chaining-plot against Dong Zhuo, leading up to his killing. Set in early 192.
Lu Bu kills Dong Zhuo (22 May 192); Li Jue/Guo Si attack Changan and kill Wang Yun (June 192).
Ma Teng marches on the capital (193); Cao Cao's father killed and Cao Cao avenges by sacking Xuzhou (autumn 193 – spring 194).
